LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT UNITED AIRLINES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT UNITED AIRLINES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THAT CAUSE OF ACTION PLEADS THAT UNITED WAS NEGLIGENT BY (1) DAMAGING HER CUSTOM BUILT BATTERY-POWERED WHEELCHAIR DURING THE FLIGHT; (2) FAILING TIMELY TO PROVIDE A SUITABLE LOANER WHEELCHAIR; (3) FAILING TO REPAIR PLAINTIFF'S WHEELCHAIR IN A TIMELY FASHION; AND (4) TELLING PLAINTIFF TO "STOP CRYING." (AC 25-28.) BECAUSE EACH THEORY OF NEGLIGENCE ALLEGES A DISTINCT BASIS FOR LIABILITY, THE COURT WILL ADDRESS THEM SEPARATELY. (SEE LILIENTHAL & FOWLER V. SUP. CRT. (1993) 12 CAL.APP.4TH 1848.) (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANT UNITED AIRLINES, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT UNITED AIRLINES, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE PRAYER FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES, PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THAT THERE IS NO STATUTORY, LEGAL OR CONTRACTUAL BASIS TO RECOVER THE FEES. THE PARTIES AGREE THAT PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE NOT RECOVERABLE ON THE NEGLIGENCE CLAIM, AND THE COURT HAS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT UNITED AIRLINES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant United Airlines, Inc.'s demurrer to the first cause of action is sustained in part and overruled in part with leave to amend. That cause of action pleads that United was negligent by (1) damaging her custom built battery-powered wheelchair during the flight; (2) failing timely to provide a suitable loaner wheelchair; (3) failing to repair Plaintiff's wheelchair in a timely fashion; and (4) telling Plaintiff to "stop crying." (AC 25-28.) Because each theory of negligence alleges a distinct basis for liability, the Court will address them separately. (See Lilienthal & Fowler v. Sup. Crt. (1993) 12 Cal.App.4th 1848.)

(See word minutes for complete details)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT UNITED AIRLINES, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant United Airlines, Inc.'s motion to strike is granted without leave to amend. As to the prayer for attorney's fees, Plaintiff concedes that there is no statutory, legal or contractual basis to recover the fees. The parties agree that punitive damages are not recoverable on the negligence claim, and the court has sustained without leave to amend Plaintiff's claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
